# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a single-page application (SPA) built with React and TypeScript, using Vite as the build tool. It follows a component-based architecture with:

- **Frontend**: React 18 with functional components and hooks
- **Routing**: React Router for client-side navigation
- **State Management**: React Query (TanStack Query) for server state, local component state for UI
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens and shadcn/ui component library
- **Backend**: Supabase providing PostgreSQL database, authentication, real-time subscriptions, and edge functions
- **Deployment**: Static site hosting (Vercel/Netlify compatible)

## Key Files and Their Purposes

### Core Application Files
- `src/main.tsx`: Application entry point, renders the root App component
- `src/App.tsx`: Main application component with routing setup
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Landing/dashboard page component

### Layout Components
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main application layout wrapper
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar
- `src/components/layout/TopBar.tsx`: Top navigation bar
- `src/components/layout/UserMenu.tsx`: User dropdown menu
- `src/components/layout/NotificationBell.tsx`: Notification indicator

### Feature Components
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Logo component with variant support
- `src/components/NavLink.tsx`: Enhanced NavLink wrapper for React Router
- `src/components/SearchableEmployeeSelect.tsx`: Employee selection dropdown with search
- `src/components/attendance/AttendanceUploadFlow.tsx`: Complex attendance file upload and parsing flow
- `src/components/employee-profile/AttendanceTab.tsx`: Employee attendance records display

### Utility Libraries
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Common utility functions (cn for className merging)
- `src/lib/attendance-format.ts`: Attendance time and duration formatting
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Role-based access control utilities
- `src/lib/notifications.ts`: Notification sending logic
- `src/lib/client-activity.ts`: Activity logging and categorization

### Integrations
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration
- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`: Generated TypeScript types for database schema

### Configuration
- `vite.config.ts`: Vite build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind CSS configuration with custom theme
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration
- `supabase/config.toml`: Supabase project configuration

## Database Schema (Key Tables)

- `employees`: Employee records with personal and job information
- `attendance_records`: Daily attendance entries with timestamps and calculations
- `leave_requests`: Leave applications and approvals
- `payroll_records`: Salary and payroll data
- `company_settings`: Organization-wide configuration
- `departments`: Department structure
- `roles`: User roles and permissions
- `projects`: Project management data
- `evaluations`: Performance review records

## Business Logic Patterns

### Attendance Processing
- File upload → AI parsing (Supabase Edge Function) → Data validation → Database insertion
- Automatic calculations: working hours, overtime, late arrivals
- Support for unmatched employee codes (stored without linking)

### Role-Based Access
- Hierarchical permissions system
- Component-level access control using `canAccess` and `hasRole` utilities
- Dynamic UI rendering based on user permissions

### Data Flow
- React Query for caching and synchronization
- Real-time subscriptions for live updates
- Optimistic updates for better UX

## Development Patterns

- **Component Structure**: Functional components with TypeScript interfaces
- **Styling**: CSS variables for theming, utility-first with Tailwind
- **Error Handling**: Toast notifications for user feedback
- **Testing**: Vitest for unit tests, Playwright for E2E
- **Code Organization**: Feature-based folder structure

## Environment Variables

- `VITE_SUPABASE_URL`: Supabase project URL
- `VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY`: Public Supabase API key

## Build and Deployment

- Development: `bun run dev`
- Build: `bun run build`
- Preview: `bun run preview`
- Testing: `bun run test`

The application is designed to be deployed as a static site with Supabase handling the backend services.
